REMARK: PETITIONER'S EX PARTE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR DUE TO NO PROOF OF SERVICE ON RESPONDENT.

REMARK: AS TO THE DEC-30-2025 EX PARTE HEARING ON PETITIONERS' APPLICATION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND RELATED RELIEF, THE COURT DENIES PETITIONERS' REQUEST ON AN EX PARTE BASIS WHICH IS RESERVED FOR EMERGENCIES IN WHICH THE RELIEF SOUGHT CANNOT BE DONE BY WAY OF A FULLY-NOTICED MOTION. THE COURT'S DENIAL IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE REQUEST BEING MADE BY WAY OF A FULLY-NOTICED HEARING THAT WOULD ALLOW FOR BRIEFING BY BOTH SIDES AND FOR THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD TO BE PROVIDED TO AND REVIEWED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (301)

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Appearances:
Edward Garcia of the Attorney General's Office (510.879.1001) for respondent and moving party; No other appearances.

The matter is called at 9:14 am and there is no appearance for the petitioner. The court hears argument on behalf of the respondent and rules as follows: DMV's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The petition for writ of mandate shows that the claims alleged therein are filed per CCP 1085, not 1094.5, and thus the claims are not time-barred. The petition does not show on its face that Karena's claim is barred by laches or that petitioners have an adequate legal remedy. DMV's argument in its reply brief that the petition seeks to compel a discretionary act that is not cognizable per 1085 is not considered because it was not addressed in the moving papers. Further, the court grants the respondent's request for judicial notice as to exhibits 1 and 2 and sustains respondent's objections to evidence in opposition of this motion. Mr. Garcia is directed to submit a proposed form of order. Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS KARENA APPLE FENG, AND LILIAN FENG'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

Appearances:
Edward Garcia of the Attorney General's Office (510.879.1001) for respondent and opposing party; No other appearances.

This matter is called at 9:14 am and there is no appearance for the petitioners. Discussed and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: denied without prejudice to being re-noticed. At the time the petitioners noticed their petition for hearing the DMV's motion for judgment on the pleadings was pending and thus the notice requesting that the petition be heard on its merits is premature and not ripe. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
